all_labels.explanation: Phase-by-phase planning guidance does not reference or shape the constraint that the itinerary must start and end in Manhattan.
Requesting a schedule based on selections does not explicitly mention where the itinerary starts/ends (Manhattan).
Stating focus on a 'relaxed Manhattan day trip' strongly aligns with (but does not explicitly assert) starting and ending in Manhattan.
Analyzing ages/parents informs pacing and activity choices, not the Manhattan start/end constraint.
Preference for quality over quantity affects itinerary density, not the Manhattan start/end requirement.
Minimizing rushing relates to pacing, not the location boundary of start/end points.
Noting commute times need refining can indirectly help ensure the itinerary stays within Manhattan, but it does not explicitly address start/end in Manhattan.
Committing to add commute times/adjust structure can support keeping the plan Manhattan-bounded, but does not directly target the start/end constraint.
Walking directions are weakly related; they might keep routing within Manhattan but do not ensure the itinerary starts/ends there.
Providing the complete Manhattan day-trip itinerary (times/activities/commutes) operationally fulfills the requirement by producing an itinerary intended to begin and conclude within Manhattan.
Booking/reservation advice does not affect whether the itinerary starts and ends in Manhattan.
Shifts focus to lunch selection nearby; sets up the decision space where 'good food' will be considered, but doesn't evaluate food quality yet.
Analyzing restaurant candidates and tradeoffs includes implicit evaluation groundwork for food quality among options.
Listing top lunch choices functions as output intended to meet the lunch requirements, including selecting for good food.
Recommending Limani is a concrete lunch recommendation aimed at satisfying the 'good food' requirement.
Refining the recommendation set continues executing the lunch recommendation output consistent with providing good-food options.
Praise for choosing Top of the Rock does not bear on lunch food quality.
Provides concrete lunch options near Rockefeller Center; this directly fulfills delivering lunch recommendations expected to have good food.
Specific Limani recommendation with reasons is direct fulfillment of a good-food lunch recommendation.
Specific La Grande Boucherie recommendation supplies another good-food lunch option, implementing the requirement.
Specific Bar Room at The Modern recommendation is an explicit good-food-oriented lunch option, implementing the requirement.
Asking for cuisine/atmosphere preferences helps tailor toward what the user considers 'good food' but does not itself fulfill it.
Acknowledging/praising the user's choice does not add or fulfill the 'good food' requirement.

Acknowledging/praising the user's lunch choice does not add, evaluate, or execute the comfortable-ambience requirement.

Option-Select: Finalizes Top of the Rock as the morning attraction; since it was offered as visually pleasant, this completes satisfaction of the requirement in the plan.
Option-Select: User confirmation reinforces the selected visually pleasant option, solidifying progress but not adding new requirement content.
Feedback-Adopt: Offering explicitly 'safe' attraction options partially satisfies the safe-area requirement by narrowing candidates to safe areas.
Feedback-Adopt: Recommending Top of the Rock with a safety framing advances selection toward meeting the safe-area requirement but does not yet finalize it.
Feedback-Adopt: Recommending The Met as safe supports meeting the safe-area requirement by proposing another compliant option.
Feedback-Adopt: Recommending a Central Park pedicab tour as a safe alternative contributes additional compliant options toward the safe-area constraint.
Option-Select: Asking the user to choose among the safe options explicitly drives the decision process to fulfill the safe-area requirement.
Intent-Reveal: Explicitly stating safety was a selection criterion directly references and evaluates against the safe-area requirement.
Option-Select: User selects an option from the presented 'safe' candidates, directly advancing fulfillment of the safe-area requirement.
Option-Select: Finalizes the morning attraction choice (Top of the Rock), which was offered as a safe-area option, thereby fulfilling the safe-area requirement for the selected attraction.
Option-Select: User confirmation locks in the chosen safe option, reinforcing fulfillment of the safe-area requirement.
